title: Air Medical 24X7: Pediatric Air Medical Evacuation from Sharjah, UAE to Addis Ababa, Ethiopia
excerpt: Air Medical 24X7 successfully conducted a complex pediatric air medical mission from Sharjah, UAE, to Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, supporting the safe transfer of a fragile, ventilator-dependent child with congenital heart disease. The operation was carried out using a fully equipped mobile NICU, ensuring continuous critical care and enabling the child’s safe return home after nearly two years of hospitalisation abroad.
content:
Air medical missions sometimes haul more than just medical equipment, charts, flight plans and preflight checklists; they bring the great weight of hope, perseverance, and a family's deepest desire to be reunited with their child.  Air medical 24X7 led the pediatric air medical evacuation from Sharjah, UAE, to Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. This is one such case where every mile flown would not only equate to the distance travelled but also a mile towards going home. 

The baby was born at a gestational age of 30 weeks, prematurely, and was born into the world facing multiple complex medical issues. Shortly thereafter, she was diagnosed with congenital heart disease and chronic respiratory failure, which required immediate and ongoing critical care. A fragile baby girl had been ventilator-dependent since February 2, 2023.

What followed was almost two years of uninterrupted hospitalisation. The NICU was where this child lived, and the medical team was an extension of her family, there for her every day, as they supported the family through numerous procedures, monitoring alarms and daily long hours in the hospital, while keeping their child alive and praying for her to return home to them.

Although her condition is extremely complicated, her family has been resolute about returning to Ethiopia, where they will be surrounded by people, language, and culture that they are familiar with, while receiving medical care closer to home. Planning a way to make her wish a reality, however, has been very complex and needed to be carried out perfectly. 

A High-Risk Transfer, Planned with Precision

Transferring a pediatric patient with ventilator dependency across international borders is one of the most complicated operations for air medical evacuation. A brief disconnection from ventilation, oxygen, or monitoring could result in serious injury or death to the child. Therefore, all elements of transferring the child across borders must be planned for and have redundancy built into them. 

Air Medical 24X7 recognised how important this operation was; therefore, they developed a comprehensive plan for pre/existing medical and aviation care before flying. They configured the aircraft to operate as a mobile NICU capable of delivering the same level of respiratory and critical care at cruising altitude, as is done in a hospital ICU. 

Air Medical 24X7 also incorporated multiple layers of redundancy, including redundant power supply, oxygen and other respiratory life-support equipment. Medical supplies were calculated based on the expected duration of the flight, but were also calculated to account for any unexpected delays. All details of the medical evacuation operation were reviewed multiple times before departure. 

Care That Never Left Her Side

Air medical 24X7 provided continuous medical care from when the child left her hospital bed in Sharjah until she was safely handed off in Addis Ababa. Bed-to-bed procedures were followed during both the initial transfer and all subsequent transfers to ensure uninterrupted continuity of medical care.

The medical configuration onboard by Air medical 24X7 included:

  • An advanced pediatric transfer ventilator for extended duration.
  • Continuous imaging of respiratory function and oxygen saturation.
  • Full aerial airway and resuscitation supplies.
  • A trained flight medic for long-distance air transfer of pediatric critical care.

Throughout the flight, Air Medical 24X7’s medical personnel closely monitored any subtle changes in the child’s respiratory function and adjusted the ventilation support in real-time. Continuous adjustments were made to the cabin altitude, the oxygen requirement, and the delicate physiology of the child with the highest regard for safety and attention to detail. The cabin of the aircraft was designed as a peaceful, controlled clinical environment; therefore, the only objective of the medical crew was to stabilise and protect the baby.

Touchdown in Addis Ababa

After hours of flying, the aeroplane arrived safely in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. The hardest part of the journey was now complete without incident. The child was stable and moved safely to the receiving hospital.

A detailed clinical handover was provided by Air Medical 24X7’s medical team to the local medical team, containing ventilator settings, recent physical examination findings, medications administered, and ongoing treatment requirements. This allowed for continuity of care, providing a seamless transition for the receiving medical staff to care for the patient.

For the family involved, this moment had extreme emotional significance. After 22 months of extensive hospital care in a foreign country, their child had finally returned to Ethiopia - safe, supported, and surrounded by the promise of the familiarity of home.

When Care Knows No Borders

This mission is the true definition of Air Medical 24X7, providing compassionate, reliable, and highly advanced air medical transfer services, without regard to geographic location, at any time.

By creating mobile NICUs and utilizing specialized pediatric critical care teams, Air Medical 24X7 eliminates the distance created between patients and highly skilled care. We are there to support families at their most vulnerable times, ensuring that even the most fragile patients can be transferred safely when ground transfer is inadequate.
author: Air Medical 24X7
